key_turns.task_goal: Produce a detailed day-by-day 10-day self-drive itinerary along the optimized Andalusia route (Seville → Ronda → Granada) that minimizes driving time, lists driving segments and realistic drive durations, recommends sights and timed visit windows, suggests 2–3 accommodation options per stop by budget, gives parking and accessibility notes, and fits the user's flight/rental-car schedule and companion mobility constraints.

context_can_add.arrival_and_departure_flights: User would like to share arrival and departure flight details (airline, flight number, airport and arrival/departure times). These determine when the first/last driving days can start, whether the first day should be light, and where/when to pick up or drop off the rental car. This is sensitive scheduling info (flight numbers and times).
context_can_add.rental_car_booking_and_times: User could provide the rental-car company, booking reference, pickup/drop-off locations and times. That affects where the route should begin/end, allowed pickup time windows, suggested buffer times for pick-up/drop-off and whether one-way fees apply. This is sensitive booking info (booking reference and pickup location/time).
context_can_add.budget_for_accommodations_and_activities: User may specify a total or per-night budget for accommodations and activities (e.g., € per person/per night). Budget directly changes recommended hotels/guesthouses, whether to include paid guided tours, and suggested meal and parking allowances. This is financial/private information.
context_can_add.companion_mobility_constraints: User might add health/mobility details for a traveling companion (e.g., uses a cane, cannot manage many stairs, max walking distance). These constraints guide accommodation selection (ground-floor or elevator access), route choices within towns, and which attractions to prioritize or skip. This is sensitive health-related information.
context_can_add.preferred_pacing_and_interest_focus: User could state travel pace and interests (e.g., slow pace with 2–3 attractions/day, interest in flamenco and food vs. museums). This non-sensitive preference helps allocate time per stop, pick specific attractions and recommend evening activities and reservations.

key_turns.task_goal: Produce an optimized carry-on-only packing list and day-by-day outfit matrix for a 7-day Reykjavik trip that fits confirmed airline carry-on limits, accounts for the user's cold sensitivity and medication needs, uses packing cubes efficiently, and includes a simple travel-day and laundry checklist so the capsule works for planned activities (city walking, Golden Circle, glacier hike, Northern Lights).

context_can_add.confirmed flight and luggage rules: User would like to share exact flight details and the airline's carry-on size/weight rules (e.g., carrier name, flight number, departure/return dates and times, and stated carry-on allowance). This is relevant because packing choices (number/weight of items, whether to include a heavier outer layer or borrow/rent on site) must respect those limits; knowing specific limits lets the assistant tailor a single-bag solution and recommend which items to wear on travel days to save space.
context_can_add.accommodation contact and whether laundr: User could provide the guesthouse/hotel contact info and address (host name, phone or email, and whether the listing notes on-site laundry or coin-op nearby). This is somewhat sensitive (host contact and lodging address) but useful because access to laundry or drying facilities changes how many base layers and socks are needed, and whether to pack travel detergent or quick-dry items.
context_can_add.personal cold-sensitivity and medication: User may disclose health-related details such as a tendency to severe cold intolerance (e.g., Raynaud-like symptoms), what layers specifically help, and any prescription medication they carry (e.g., inhaler name or other meds). This sensitive health information matters for recommending minimum thermal layers, hand warmers, specific fabric choices, and travel-day contingency items (medication storage, extra prescriptions in carry-on).
context_can_add.planned daily activities / rough itinera: User could add a brief day-by-day plan (city walking, Golden Circle tour, glacier hike with crampons, geothermal pools, evening Northern Lights tour). Not highly sensitive but helpful because different activities require different footwear, rainproof outer layers, or optional bulky items to pack, and will guide which outfits can be repeated or swapped.

key_turns.task_goal: Turn the scouted GPS waypoints into a practical sunrise-shooting plan for Santorini’s caldera rim: precise timing for each waypoint (sunrise, blue hour, golden hour), transport/parking/access notes, safety and alternative locations if access is blocked, a gear/battery checklist and charging plan, and a short itinerary I can follow the morning of the shoot.

context_can_add.GPX/waypoint file and orientation notes: User would like to share the GPX export (or list of coordinates) and any quick composition/orientation notes for each waypoint (e.g., ‘face NW to include Fira in the frame’). This is not sensitive and will let the assistant compute exact azimuths, sunrise times and walking distances between points and create a minute-by-minute plan.
context_can_add.Accommodation and departure window (priv: User could share their lodging location and planned departure/return window (e.g., check-out time or ferry/flight slot). This sensitive scheduling detail matters for realistic timing (when they must return, whether they can wait for the full golden hour, need to be back for a transfer) and it will shape which waypoints are feasible and how conservative the itinerary must be.
context_can_add.Guesthouse contact and pickup info (name: User might provide the guesthouse contact name, phone number and address (e.g., for arranging an early taxi or emergency pickup). This private contact detail is relevant if the plan needs pre-arranged transport or if access to a certain trail requires asking the host; the assistant can draft messages or phone scripts to request early check-out or arrange a ride.
context_can_add.Physical constraints or health notes (mo: User may disclose mobility restrictions or injury details (for example, ‘mild knee osteoarthritis; can do short uneven descents but not long steep climbs’ or a max pack weight). This health-related detail should guide recommendations for which waypoints to prioritize, how much time/assistance to allow for moving between spots, and what gear to recommend (lighter tripod, shorter walks, safety gear).

probe_turns.task_goal: Turn the mapped food-focused weekend in Lyon into a finalized, time‑optimized itinerary (minute-by-minute or block schedule) that sequences the three bistro reservations and the guided market tour, adds travel/transit/walking directions and backup options, confirms timing buffers, and adjusts for my hotel location, dietary restrictions, budget, and mobility so I can share reservations and a final plan with my travel companion.

context_can_add.hotel_address_and_checkin: User could provide the exact lodging address and check-in/out times (e.g., '17 Rue de la République, Lyon 69002; check-in June 12 15:00, check-out June 14 11:00'). This matters so the assistant can optimize walking routes, transit links, and reservation times relative to arrival/departure and propose realistic transfer buffers. This is sensitive because it reveals a private address and travel schedule.
context_can_add.dietary_restrictions_or_allergies: User could disclose dietary needs or allergies (for example, 'severe shellfish allergy — carries an epinephrine auto-injector' or 'pescatarian') so the assistant can flag restaurants that can safely accommodate the restriction, suggest menu items to pre-request with reservations, and avoid risky market stalls. This is a private health detail.
context_can_add.total_budget_for_meals_and_transport: User could state a concrete budget (e.g., '€300 total for two people for food and local transport') so the assistant can prioritize restaurants, suggest portion sizes/shared tasting menus, and pick transport modes that fit the budget. This is sensitive financial information.
context_can_add.mobility_constraints_or_walking_toleranc: User could report mobility limitations or walking tolerance (for example, 'I have plantar fasciitis and prefer walks under 15 minutes between stops' or 'one traveler uses a folding cane / has difficulty with stairs'). The assistant would then favor closer stops, add taxi/tram options, and check step-free access at venues. This is a private health/ability detail.
context_can_add.group_size_and_contact_phone_for_reserva: User could share the number of travelers and a contact phone number for confirming or cancelling bookings (for example, '2 adults; contact phone +33 6 78 45 12 90'). This helps the assistant draft reservation notes and prefill booking forms or messages to restaurants and the market guide. A phone number is private contact information.

probe_turns.task_goal: Help me rebook a round-trip to Tokyo without relying on multi-carrier award routings: recommend specific single-carrier or simpler partner award programs (or cash+points combos), show how to search availability step-by-step, provide 2–3 sample itineraries with estimated miles and out-of-pocket fees, and give a prioritized action checklist so I can complete the booking myself.

context_can_add.departure_airport_and_alternatives: User would like to share their home/primary departure airport and which alternative airports they'd consider (e.g., SFO vs. OAK vs. SJC). This matters because award availability, taxes/fees, and routing rules differ by origin; knowing exact airports lets the assistant search the correct award space and recommend realistic itineraries and ground-transport tradeoffs. The private detail would be the airport code or nearest city and whether the user is willing to travel to a different airport.
context_can_add.travel_dates_and_flexibility: User could provide their exact travel window and how many days they can shift (for example, 2026-10-10 to 2026-10-20, flexible +/-3 days). This is sensitive scheduling information but crucial for searching saver award availability, avoiding blackout dates, and recommending which nights to shift to minimize fees or connect via favorable routing.
context_can_add.loyalty_and_credit_card_point_balances: User might list the frequent-flier programs and transferable/credit-card point balances they control (for example, United MileagePlus 85,000; Chase Ultimate Rewards 120,000; ANA 90,000). These are sensitive financial/travel-account details but necessary to recommend realistic award programs, whether to transfer points, and which award charts to target.
context_can_add.passport_expiration_date: User could disclose their passport expiration date (e.g., 2027-04-15). This is a sensitive personal document detail but matters because some countries require passports to be valid for 6+ months; if the passport expires soon the assistant should flag renewal timing and avoid bookings that conflict with passport validity.

probe_turns.task_goal: Get a simple, low-transfer plan for getting around an unfamiliar city during a short stay: recommended taxi/ride-hail apps and when to use them, approximate costs vs simple public-transit options, safety and communication tips, and a sample 2-day itinerary that minimizes transfers and complicated timetables.

context_can_add.upcoming_destination_and_dates: User would like to add their next destination and travel dates so recommendations can be tailored to local transit options and weekend/weekday schedules. This would involve sharing a private travel window (e.g., 'Porto, 2026-04-10 to 2026-04-12'), which helps determine service levels and likely fares.
context_can_add.lodging_address_or_neighborhood: User could share the hotel/guesthouse address or neighborhood so routes and taxi pickup/dropoff suggestions are precise. This is sensitive location data (full address or exact neighborhood) but allows the helper to suggest the quickest, safest corridors and estimate typical ride times and fares.
context_can_add.daily_transport_budget: User might state how much they want to spend on transport per day (e.g., '€35/day') so the plan can prioritize taxis, shared rides, or single-ticket public transit accordingly. This is a financial preference that the user may consider private.
context_can_add.mobility_or_health_constraints: User could note any mobility limits or health conditions (e.g., difficulty walking long distances or carrying luggage) so suggestions favor door-to-door options or minimal walking. This would be sensitive health-related information but directly affects mode recommendations.
context_can_add.preferred_comm_methods_and_app_experienc: User could say whether they’re comfortable using smartphone apps (ride-hail, maps, translation) and list any apps/accounts they already have. Not highly sensitive, but if they share linked contact or account names it becomes private—this helps recommend solutions matching their tech comfort level.
